name: Local Falcon
description:
What Local Falcon does

Local Falcon pioneered grid‑style rank tracking in 2018 and now offers advanced AI‑driven analysis. The platform introduces the Share of Local Voice (SoLV) metric, which measures how often a business appears in Google’s map pack across different radii. It includes multiple grid sizes, AI analysis with recommendations, customizable grid point density, and support for both Google and Apple Maps.

Key Features

  • Customizable geo‑grid tracking for multiple keywords and locations.
  • SoLV metric shows how frequently your listings show up in the Map Pack.
  • Falcon AI analysis & recommendations
  • AI visibility tracking across AI search engines
  • Google & Apple Maps support
  • Integrates with Looker Studio and Zapier.

Benefits of Local Falcon

  • The SoLV metric reframes success around map‑pack visibility rather than absolute ranking.
  • Multiple grid sizes and customizable densities provide granular control over where to look for ranking gaps.
  • Visualizations make it easier to demonstrate local ranking gaps to potential clients and justify your services. 
  • Support for Google and Apple Maps ensures that your client’s presence is monitored across both major local platforms.
  • Falcon AI analyzes local search data to provide tailored recommendations, and Falcon Assist offers conversational guidance and answers local SEO questions.
  • AI visibility tracking helps you track presence across all major AI search platforms; you can use it for generative engine optimization (GEO).

Pricing

Local Falcon offers several packages using a credit system. Plans start at $24.99/month (Starter, 90,000 credits), with Basic ($49.99), Pro ($99.99), and Premium ($199.99) options for higher credit allowances. 

A free trial with 100 credits is available.

Pros

  • Innovative SoLV metric and AI‑powered recommendations.
  • Multiple grid sizes and densities for granular analysis.
  • White‑label reporting that includes multi-point map grids.
  • Integrations with Looker Studio and Zapier for data visualization and automation.

Cons

  • Pricing can go up if you need a lot of credits; heavy users may find it more expensive than expected. 
  • Occasional API lags may lead to rare misinformation in reports.
  • Limited features beyond rank tracking (no listings management or reputation tools).
what_people_say:
“I like the trend report that shows the progress that my clients are making for their target keywords in Google Maps. As the SoLV increases, so does the number of impressions, website clicks, phone calls, and leads. I also appreciate how the tool is expanding to include ChatGPT / Google Gemini, as that is at the top of my mind for 2025.”

— Review collected by and hosted on G2.com
summing_up_description:
Summing Up…

White‑label rank trackers allow agencies to present professional, branded reporting while accessing accurate data behind the scenes. When choosing a tool, evaluate the balance between features, scalability, and price. 

Synup stands out for agencies needing hyper‑local grid tracking alongside a full agency operating system. SE Ranking offers a cost‑effective all‑rounder for agencies that also need keyword research, site audits, and backlink monitoring, while BrightLocal is ideal for multi‑location brands with large sets of locations and stakeholders.

‍

Get Started with Synup

If you need a rank tracker that does more than plot points on a map, take Synup into consideration. You get grid and keyword rank tracking, automated reporting, and a white‑label client portal within a broader agency OS. The ability to manage listings, reviews, social posts, and CRM tasks from one dashboard means fewer subscriptions and less context switching.

‍

FAQs

  1. How often should I check keyword rankings? 

It depends on campaign goals. Most agencies schedule daily or weekly scans to catch sudden ranking changes and monitor ongoing SEO efforts. Tools like Synup and GMB Radar offer automated scheduling so you can essentially set it and forget it.

  1. Is white‑labeling necessary for small agencies? 

White‑labeling adds professionalism and trust, but it’s not mandatory. If you work with a few clients and communicate results personally, a standard rank tracker may suffice. However, as your agency grows, white‑label dashboards reduce repetitive reporting and keep your brand front‑of‑mind.

‍
